header.title: Sales SOP
header.subtitle: Finding and serving clients, taking orders, applying MGC pricing, and post-sales follow-up across the Commercial, Bulk, Dealer, Semi-dealer, and Overall channels.

stages.english: Find Client | Serve | Schedule | Repeat Order
sop_steps: Call client for re-orders, or find new client orders. (S1A1.1)
System auto-reminds the client and Sales Manager ahead of an impending order based on forecast. (S1A1.2)
Create a draft Sales Order, Pick List, and Delivery Receipt for each order. (S1A1.3)
Check Sales History for upcoming re-orders. (S1A1.4)
Review current and upcoming inventory levels for upselling opportunities. (S1A1.5)
Review next week's route schedule for upselling opportunities. (S1A1.6)
Confirm the Sales Order and Invoice; auto-assign a permanent, non-editable Sales Order Control No. (S1A2.1-S1A2.3)
Update the synced Operations Calendar for current deliveries. (S1A2.4)
Assign and log notes, activities, and tags for each Sales Order. (S1A2.5)
Post-sales: evaluate Sales Target vs Actual Sales; sync with the Operations Calendar and actual delivery. (S1A3.1-S1A3.2)
Real-time computation of gains/losses per trip, per driver/helper and back-office team. (S1A3.3)
Record sales-fulfilment concerns/issues and log Accounting-related notes. (S1A3.4-S1A3.5)
guidelines: Sales Order Control Numbers are permanent and non-editable once assigned - a wrong entry is corrected with a new document, never by editing the control number.
The upsell review (inventory levels, route schedule) happens before the order is finalized, as a scheduled step in the flow, not an optional add-on afterward.
Gains/loss variance per trip is computed against the Operations Calendar in real time; unresolved fulfilment concerns are logged, never left unrecorded.

approval_matrix.approver: AVP Sales (reviews upcoming orders to upsell)

current: System Features such as auto-reminders and real-time dashboards are tagged active in the source, but several Data Output fields are still marked placeholder - dashboard/report wiring may be incomplete for some outputs; verify go-live status per feature with the Sales Manager.
future: No further automation items were flagged for Commercial beyond the active Odoo rollout; treat full completion of the still-placeholder fields as the near-term target rather than a new build.

stages.english: Find Client | Serve | Schedule | Repeat Order
sop_steps: Order intake, pricing, and confirmation follow the same Odoo-based process as Sales > Commercial (S1A1-S1A3), per company specification ("same as Commercial sales avenue, with exceptions").
Orders are sized to fill bulk lorry capacity (8-ton truck) rather than individual cylinder counts - see Operations > Bulk for the fulfilment side.
Bulk-specific pricing and exception handling are referenced but not yet documented in either source.
guidelines: Sales > Bulk explicitly inherits Commercial's intake/approval flow per company note; the undocumented "exceptions" must be captured before this is treated as final SOP, particularly around bulk-specific pricing tiers and truck-capacity minimums.

current: Bulk sales handled as an unexceptioned copy of the Commercial flow; no bulk-specific pricing or approval variance is on file.
future: Document the specific bulk exceptions (pricing tier, minimum load, dedicated approval step if any) and encode them as a distinct Sales > Bulk procedure rather than an inherited one.

stages.gap_note: The source site explicitly flags this stage as "Same as Commercial sales avenue, with exceptions" but never lists the exceptions, and the process manual does not cover Sales > Bulk separately. Recommend a short session with the Regional Sales Manager to capture what's actually different about Bulk pricing/approval before finalizing.
stages.sources: Source site (wilsonsykes.github.io/MGSDiagramWorkflow) - Sales > Bulk (placeholder). No corresponding section in "MGC LPG Process Manual v2.docx".

stages.english: Find Client | Serve | Schedule | Repeat Order
sop_steps: Sales Assistant coordinates the dealer's Plant Visit Calendar in advance, contacting dealers roughly three times a week.
Claire sends schedules and updates through the Viber Dealer Group.
Dealer identity and authorization are verified; only confirmed appointments are accommodated.
Confirm the dealer maintains at least 50 cylinders on hand; dealers typically buy in larger quantities.
Dealer advance quantity requests/orders are disseminated to multiple staff; the dealer database is kept current. (S3A1.3-S3A1.4)
Dealer brings empty cylinders for refilling or swapping (typically 8 tons).
Apply dealer pricing from the MGC Pricing Matrix, adjusted within the per-dealer buffer.
Set payment terms - installment with Post-Dated Checks (PDCs), COD, or a Promissory Note if no PDC is available.
Produce the digital Sales Order and its counterpart Invoice, editable before confirmation; produce LUT, EES and other documents for printing. (S3A2.1-S3A2.2)
Confirm the order - triggers instant Sales/Inventory/Invoice movement on a shared, multi-user dashboard. (S3A2.3-S3A2.4)
Fulfil: Mel schedules the plant visit/delivery and coordinates truck availability.
Post-sales: Claire maintains dealer records, runs call-outs and follow-ups, and handles pricing disputes/adjustments. (S3A3)

Cylinder swapping, marketing support, and pricing-adjustment requests route through: Sales Assistant -> Sir RY -> Warehouse and Quality Supervisor -> Senior Supervisor -> Operations Officer. (S3A3.1-S3A3.5)
New Dealer Accreditation: applicant proposes a business location with no existing dealer coverage.
Once approved, secure DTI Registration, Business Permit, and BIR Registration.
Submit the dealership application and secure the Supplier's Certificate.
Complete DOE requirements (Annex G and Annex V5) and execute a Special Power of Attorney (SPA).
Pay processing fees and submit documents to the Makati Office; coordinate with the DOE liaison for processing.
guidelines: Only confirmed, scheduled dealer appointments are accommodated - mirrors the plant-refilling rule in Operations > Dealer.
New dealers must maintain a minimum of 50 LPG cylinders; MGC may process DOE/permit requirements on the applicant's behalf, or the applicant may self-file.
Cylinder swapping, marketing support, and new pricing adjustments all route through the same five-step approval chain ending in Operations Officer sign-off - no skipping to a later approver.
Dealer pricing is adjusted only within the per-dealer buffer defined in the MGC Pricing Matrix; adjustments outside the buffer require the same escalation used for a swap request.

approval_matrix.transaction: Cylinder swap / marketing support / pricing concern
approval_matrix.approver: Sir RY, then Operations Officer logs

current: Swap and pricing-concern approvals still route through ad hoc chat threads rather than a structured workflow.
Dealer accreditation paperwork is tracked manually across DTI/BIR/DOE filings with no single status view.
future: Extend the real-time, multi-user dashboard already covering order/invoice/inventory movement to swap approvals and accreditation status tracking.
Digital CRM for the new-dealer application process, start to end (already flagged as a target system feature).

stages.sources: Source site (wilsonsykes.github.io/MGSDiagramWorkflow) - Sales > Dealer (S3A1-S3A3); "MGC LPG Process Manual v2.docx" - Sales > Dealer (Order Process & Dealer Accreditation).

stages.english: Find Client | Serve | Schedule | Repeat Order
sop_steps: Solicit and plan orders against daily and monthly Semi-dealer sales targets.
Call clients for re-orders and find new client orders; review sales history and next week's route schedule.
Take the order: confirm the Sales Order and assign a permanent, non-editable Sales Order Control No.
Semi-dealer and semi-dealer clients buy smaller quantities, mainly 11kg and 2.5kg, on a Semi-dealer scale.
Apply pricing: offer SRP first, then the Minimum Price List (MPL) with volume discounts (3-5, 6-10 units) and distance-zone pricing (0-30km, 30.1-40km, 41-60km).
Semi-dealer customers may submit a portal order (with discount); Sales Assistant approves against schedule and payment terms, Sales Manager validates. (S4A1)
Set payment: mostly COD/cash invoice; the delivery team carries the cash invoice.
Fulfil: Sales Assistant lines up confirmed Sales Orders; Purchaser/Dispatcher reviews the lined-up orders and routes. (S4A2)
Post-sales: log concerns, evaluate sales target vs actual, and sync with the Operations calendar.
Compute gains/losses per trip per driver/helper; record customer/location/category sales contribution.
guidelines: Semi-dealer pricing always follows SRP first, then falls to the Minimum Price List with volume/zone discounts - MPL is the floor, not the opening offer.
Sales Order Control Numbers are permanent and non-editable, the same rule as Commercial.
Semi-dealer is COD/cash-invoice by default; the delivery team physically carries the cash invoice and reconciles it same-day with Accounting > Semi-dealer.

current: Semi-dealer order-to-cash is still carried physically via cash invoice, reconciled same-day rather than in real time.
Several Data Output fields for Semi-dealer delivery scheduling are marked placeholder in the source and appear unfinished.
future: Extend the Commercial/Dealer real-time dashboard pattern to Semi-dealer so cash-invoice status is visible before the driver returns, not just at end-of-day reconciliation.

stages.sources: Source site (wilsonsykes.github.io/MGSDiagramWorkflow) - Sales > Semi-dealer (S4A1-S4A2); "MGC LPG Process Manual v2.docx" - Sales > Semi-dealer.

sop_steps: Print and verify multi-period Sales Performance Reports (daily/weekly/monthly, per sales avenue). (S5A1)
Report covers invoice status and incentive gains/loss; issue Invoice-linked Credit Notes for Commercial clients and the delivery team. (S5A1.3)

IT Head (Jabert) prints the Sales Performance Report (SPR) bi-weekly or monthly depending on inventory volume; fields include sales per kg, total sales, net sales, cost of sales, and Gross Profit Margin per kg and total.
Regional Sales Manager (Mel) sets up the MGC LPG Pricing Matrix for decision; Sir RY decides on the matrix for the period, 1-3 times per month in a General Meeting. (S5A2)
Pricing Matrix covers Sales Price, MPL, and Total Cost (Acquisition Cost + Hauling) per kg, by avenue - Commercial through Semi-dealer and semi-dealers - plus MPL volume discounts by radius.
Regional Sales Manager and IT Head update competitor end-user pricing (SRP comparison).
Price Cascade Process: Regional Sales Manager emails the updated changes; IT Head records the audit log for the change.

IT Head informs CODO, contact centers, marketing, and cashiers. Cascade is channel-owned: Regional Sales Manager and Sales Assistant1 - Commercial/Bulk clients (Mel, Lou); Sales Assistant2 - Semi-dealer clients (Claire); AVP Sales - Makati accounts (Jen); Jabert - CODO/audit and contact centers/marketing/cashiers.
Sir RY sets the Sales Quota per client per period; AVP Sales prints and uses it as the working target; Sales Assistant updates it daily/weekly. (S5A3)
guidelines: Price changes are decided only in the General Meeting (1-3x per month) by Sir RY - no ad hoc price change cascades without going through this meeting.
Every price change is audit-logged by the IT Head before it cascades to any client-facing channel.

Cascade responsibility is channel-specific and does not overlap: Mel/Lou (Commercial/Bulk), Claire (Semi-dealer), Jen (Makati), Jabert (CODO/contact centers/marketing/cashiers) - a price update is not company-wide live until all channels confirm cascade.

current: Price cascade is confirmed via email and informal notification to several separate people, with no single cascade-complete checklist.
Sales Quota tracking has several Data Output fields still marked placeholder in the source.
future: A single Pricing Matrix dashboard (already partially specified) that shows cascade status per channel instead of relying on the Regional Sales Manager's memory of who's been notified.

stages.sources: Source site (wilsonsykes.github.io/MGSDiagramWorkflow) - Sales > Overall (S5A1-S5A3, S5C1-S5C2); Personnel & Forms indexes for named roles.

cross_stage.name: Cross-Stage Governance
cross_stage.description: Escalation and pricing-governance rules that apply on top of every Sales approval row above.
guidelines: Sales Order Control Numbers, once assigned, are permanent and non-editable; a correction is made with a new document, never by editing the original control number.
Price changes are decided only in the scheduled General Meeting (1-3x per month) by Sir RY, and every change is audit-logged by the IT Head before it cascades to any client-facing channel.

Any transaction blocked or disputed at its normal approval level escalates through the standard chain (System watch -> Review -> Manager) rather than being resolved informally between the two parties involved.

footer: Masagana Gas Corporation - Sales SOP - Merged from Process Systemization Portal + source site - Internal use only
